is a Folio number?","description":[{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"Much like a bank account number, a Folio number is your Account Number in a Mutual Fund Scheme, under which yours Unit holdings in a mutual fund scheme are recorded in the Unit Holders’ Register.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"","type":"text"}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"Most Mutual Funds allot a Master Folio number, so that Unit holdings of a unit holder (or same set of unit holders, in case of joint holders) are shown under a common folio number thereby avoiding the need to remember multiple folio numbers.","type":"text"}]}]},{"__component":"content.accordion","id":2083,"title":"What is an Account Statement?","description":[{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"As the name suggests, it is a statement that reflects your holding in a scheme. A statement of accounts is like a bank pass book.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"","type":"text"}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"An account statement will reflect the following","type":"text"}]},{"type":"list","format":"unordered","children":[{"type":"list-item","children":[{"text":"The scheme in which you have invested.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"list-item","children":[{"text":"The amount you've invested, the purchase price and the units you were allotted.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"list-item","children":[{"text":"Other details like Bank details, mailing and contact details, nominee details etc.","type":"text"}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"The Mutual Funds issue a Consolidated Account Statement (CAS) across all fund houses once a month. However, one may request for an account statement with each fund house that will solely reflect the holdings in the schemes managed by that particular fund house. There may be charges levied for issue of a duplicate account statement.","type":"text"}]}]},{"__component":"content.accordion","id":2084,"title":"When does the investor get an account statement after investing in a mutual fund?","description":[{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"Mutual funds are required to issue statements of accounts within five working days from the date of closure of the initial subscription of the scheme. In case of close-ended schemes, the investors would get either a demat account statement as these are traded in

fund houses once a month. However, one may request for an account statement with each fund house that will solely reflect the holdings in the schemes managed by that particular fund house. There may be charges levied for issue of a duplicate account statement.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2084,\"title\":\"When does the investor get an account statement after investing in a mutual fund?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Mutual funds are required to issue statements of accounts within five working days from the date of closure of the initial subscription of the scheme. In case of close-ended schemes, the investors would get either a demat account statement as these are traded in the stock exchanges. In case of open-ended schemes, a statement of account is issued by the mutual fund within five working days from the date of closure of initial public offer of the scheme and/or from the date of receipt of the request from the unitholders. The procedure of repurchase is mentioned in the offer document.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Also, AMCs are required to send confirmation specifying the number of units allotted to the applicant by way of email and/or SMS’s to the applicant’s registered email address and/or mobile number as soon as possible but not later than five working days from the date of closure of the initial subscription list and/or from the date of receipt of the request from the unitholders.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2085,\"title\":\"What are cut-off times?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"The Cut-off timing is the time before which an investor has to submit a valid purchase or withdrawal/ redemption form to be eligible for a particular day's price or Net Asset Value (NAV).\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2086,\"title\":\"What is NAV ?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"The performance of a particular scheme of a mutual fund is denoted by Net Asset Value (NAV). In simple words, NAV is the market value of the securities held by the scheme.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Mutual funds invest the money collected from investors in securities markets. Since market value of securities changes every day, NAV of a scheme also varies on day to day basis.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"The NAV per unit is the market value of securities of a scheme divided by the total number of units of the scheme on any particular date.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"For example, if the market value of securities of a mutual fund scheme is INR 200 lakh and the mutual fund has issued 10 lakh units of INR 10 each to the investors, then the NAV per unit of the fund is INR 20 (i.e.200 lakh/10 lakh). NAV is required to be disclosed by the mutual funds on a daily basis. The NAV per unit of all mutual fund schemes have to be updated on AMFIís website and the Mutual Funds’ website by 9 p.m. of the same day. Fund of Funds are allowed time till 10 a.m. the following business day to update the information. For details on Disclosure of NAV of Mutual fund schemes, please \",\"type\":\"text\"},{\"url\":\"https://www.amfiindia.com/uploads/Review_of_time_limit_for_disclosure_of_NAV_of_Mutual_fund_schemes_f279ff1a8b.pdf\",\"type\":\"link\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"click here\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Unlike stocks (where the price is driven by the market and changes from minute-to-minute) , mutual funds don't declare NAVs through the day.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Instead, NAVs of all mutual fund schemes are declared at the end of the trading day after markets are closed, in accordance with SEBI Mutual Fund Regulations. Further, as per SEBI Mutual Fund Regulations, for all mutual fund schemes, other than liquid fund schemes, the mutual fund Units are allotted only at prospective NAV, i.e., the NAV that would be declared at the end of the day, based on the closing market value of the securities held in the respective schemes.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Thus, what is important here is the cut-off time for submission/receipt of the transaction – If you invest before the cut-off time, you will get the end-of-day NAV of that particular business day. The cut off time for

are closed, in accordance with SEBI Mutual Fund Regulations. Further, as per SEBI Mutual Fund Regulations, for all mutual fund schemes, other than liquid fund schemes, the mutual fund Units are allotted only at prospective NAV, i.e., the NAV that would be declared at the end of the day, based on the closing market value of the securities held in the respective schemes.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Thus, what is important here is the cut-off time for submission/receipt of the transaction – If you invest before the cut-off time, you will get the end-of-day NAV of that particular business day. The cut off time for purchase transactions for all mutual fund schemes other than liquid fund schemes is 3:00 p.m. This means that if you have invested till 3:00 p.m. on a particular day, you will get that day's NAV.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"A mutual fund may accept applications even after the cut-off time, but you will get the NAV of the next business day. Further, the cut-off time rules apply for redemptions too. \",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2087,\"title\":\"How is the applicable NAV determined?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"As per SEBI circular no. SEBI/HO/IMD/DF2/CIR/P/2020/175 dated September 17, 2020 read with circular no. SEBI/HO/IMD/DF2/CIR/P/2020/253 dated December 31, 2020, effective from February 1, 2021, the applicable NAV in respect of purchase of units of mutual fund scheme shall be subject to realization \u0026 availability of the funds in the bank account of mutual fund before the applicable cut off timings for purchase transactions, irrespective of the amount of investment, under ALL mutual fund schemes.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"bold\":true,\"text\":\"Liquid Funds\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"- Subscription\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list\",\"format\":\"unordered\",\"children\":[{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Where the application is received up to 1.30 p.m. on a day and funds are available for utilization before 1.30 p.m. without availing any credit facility, the closing NAV of the day immediately preceding the day of receipt of application.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Where the application is received after 1.30 p.m. on a day and funds are available for utilization on the same day without availing any credit facility, the closing NAV of the day immediately preceding the next business day; and\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Irrespective of the time of receipt of application (before or after 1.30 p.m. on a day), where the funds are not available for utilization before 1.30 p.m. without availing any credit facility, the closing NAV of the day immediately preceding the day on which the funds are available for utilization.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"- Redemption\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list\",\"format\":\"unordered\",\"children\":[{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Where the application is received up to 3.00 pm – the closing NAV of day immediately preceding the next business day; and\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Where the application is received after 3.00 pm – the closing NAV of the next business day./li\u003e\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"bold\":true,\"text\":\"Other Schemes [other than Liquid Funds]\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"a. Subscription\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list\",\"format\":\"unordered\",\"children\":[{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Where the application is received up to 3:00 p.m. and funds are available for utilization before 3:00 p.m., closing NAV of the day on which the application is received.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Where the application is received after 3:00 p.m. and funds are available for utilization, closing NAV of the next business day.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Irrespective of the time of receipt of application (before or after 3:00 p.m.), where the funds are not available for utilization, closing NAV of the day on which the funds are available for utilization.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"b. Redemption\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list\",\"format\":\"unordered\",\"children\":[{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Where the application is received up to 3.00 pm – closing NAV of the day on which the application is received; and\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Where the application is received after 3.00 pm – closing NAV of the next business day.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2088,\"title\":\"Where can one get information on NAVs of mutual funds?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"All mutual funds publish their scheme NAVs on their respective web sites.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"In addition, one can also access the NAVs of all mutual funds on the web site of Association of Mutual Funds in India (AMFI) at \",\"type\":\"text\"},{\"url\":\"https://www.amfiindia.com\",\"type\":\"link\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"www.amfiindia.com.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2089,\"title\":\"What is the purpose / rationale for cut-off times for purchases and redemption's?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Mutual funds are market linked investments. They invest in either

is received up to 3.00 pm – closing NAV of the day on which the application is received; and\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Where the application is received after 3.00 pm – closing NAV of the next business day.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2088,\"title\":\"Where can one get information on NAVs of mutual funds?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"All mutual funds publish their scheme NAVs on their respective web sites.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"In addition, one can also access the NAVs of all mutual funds on the web site of Association of Mutual Funds in India (AMFI) at \",\"type\":\"text\"},{\"url\":\"https://www.amfiindia.com\",\"type\":\"link\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"www.amfiindia.com.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2089,\"title\":\"What is the purpose / rationale for cut-off times for purchases and redemption's?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Mutual funds are market linked investments. They invest in either Equity, Bond or Gold markets and these markets have fixed open and closing hours for trading. The NAV of a scheme is based market value of the underlying portfolio and thus highly correlated with the market prices. So in order to be eligible for a particular days' NAV, the fund manager needs to be aware of the amount that needs to be purchased or sold as per the settlement period and trading times of the markets. The timings also largely help in ensuring that all classes of investors get the same treatment irrespective of their quantum of investing\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2090,\"title\":\"What is sale and repurchase/redemption price?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"The price or NAV a unit holder is charged while investing in a mutual fund scheme is called sales price.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Repurchase or redemption price is the price or NAV at which a mutual fund repurchases (buys back) / redeems its units from the unitholders. It may include exit load, if applicable.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2091,\"title\":\"What is Total Expense Ratio?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Expense ratio represents the annual fund operating expenses of a scheme, expressed as a percentage of the fund’s daily net assets. Operating expenses of a scheme are administration, management, advertising related expenses, etc.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"An expense ratio of 1% per annum means that each year 1% of the fund’s total assets will be used to cover expenses. Information on expense ratio that may be applicable to a scheme is mentioned in the offer document. Currently, in India, the expense ratio is fungible, i.e., there is no limit on any particular type of allowed expense as long as the total expense ratio is within the prescribed limit. For limits on expense ratio, refer to regulation 52 of the SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 1996.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2092,\"title\":\"Do I need to pay any fees to the distributor who sells me mutual fund schemes?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"As per SEBI mutual Fund Regulations, no entry load can be charged for any mutual fund scheme. An investor may choose to pay fees to a distributor based on the investor’s assessment of various factors including the service rendered by the distributor. However, for investments made through a distributor, commission is paid directly by AMC to the distributor within the limits on expense ratio specified under regulation 52 of the SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations, 1996. Hence, the cost borne by investors remains within the limit prescribed under SEBI Regulations.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Transaction Charges:\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Mutual Fund Distributors are permitted to recover transaction charges of ₹150 from a new investor and ₹100 per subscription transaction of ₹10,000 from an existing investor.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"This transaction charge can be levied only if a distributor has opted in to levy transaction charge for that type of mutual fund scheme. Further, the transaction charge, if any, are deducted by the mutual fund from the subscription amount and passed on to the distributor and the balance amount is invested.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2093,\"title\":\"How does an investor know how much commission the distributor is earning and what is the TER being charged in a scheme?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"From October 1, 2016, the monthly Consolidated Account Statement (CAS) issued to investors is required to provide information in terms of total purchase value/cost of

from an existing investor.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"This transaction charge can be levied only if a distributor has opted in to levy transaction charge for that type of mutual fund scheme. Further, the transaction charge, if any, are deducted by the mutual fund from the subscription amount and passed on to the distributor and the balance amount is invested.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2093,\"title\":\"How does an investor know how much commission the distributor is earning and what is the TER being charged in a scheme?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"From October 1, 2016, the monthly Consolidated Account Statement (CAS) issued to investors is required to provide information in terms of total purchase value/cost of investment in each scheme. Further, CAS issued for the half-year (ended September/March) shall also provide the amount of actual commission paid by AMCs/Mutual Funds to distributors (in absolute terms) during the half-year period against the concerned investor’s total investments in each Mutual Fund scheme.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"The term ‘commission’ here refers to all direct monetary payments and other payments made in the form of gifts/rewards, trips, event sponsorships etc. by AMCs/MFs to distributors. The commission disclosed in the HY-CAS is the gross commission and does not exclude costs incurred by distributors such as service tax (wherever applicable, as per existing rates), operating expenses, etc.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2094,\"title\":\"What is ASBA?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"ASBA is an acronym for application supported by blocked amounts and is a facility provided by banks to investors in New Fund Offers (NFOs) of mutual funds. If you apply for Units via ASBA during NFO, your application amount is blocked in your bank account and debited only upon allotment of Units. In other words, the amount blocked remains in your bank account till allotment, but it cannot be used until allotment is completed.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"For more information, refer to FAQs on ASBA available on SEBI website at \",\"type\":\"text\"},{\"url\":\"http://www.sebi.gov.in/cms/sebi_data/commondocs/asbaprocess1_p.pdf\",\"type\":\"link\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"http://www.sebi.gov.in/cms/sebi_data/commondocs/asbaprocess1_p.pdf\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"text\":\".\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2095,\"title\":\"What is a Direct Plan?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"One may invest in mutual funds with the help of a financial intermediary i.e., a Mutual Fund distributor/agent in a Regular Plan OR choose to INVEST DIRECTLY i.e., without involving or routing the investment through any distributor/agent in a ‘Direct Plan’.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"'Direct Plan' and 'Regular Plan’ are both part of the same mutual fund scheme, have the same / common portfolio and are managed by the same fund manager, but have different expense ratios (recurring expenses that is incurred by the mutual fund scheme). Direct Plan has lower expense ratio than the Regular Plan, as there is no distributor/agent involved, and hence there is saving in terms of distribution cost/commissions paid out to the distributor/agent, which is added back to the returns of the scheme. Hence, a Direct Plan has a separate NAV, which is higher than the normal “Regular” Plan’s NAV.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"To know more about Direct Plan, \",\"type\":\"text\"},{\"url\":\"/investor/knowledge-center-info?zoneName=DirectPlan\",\"type\":\"link\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"click here\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2096,\"title\":\"Can I invest in units in cash?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Yes, cash investments up to ₹50,000 per investor, per mutual fund, per financial year are permitted. However, the repurchase or dividend payment is not made in cash, but paid by cheque / draft or direct credit to investor’s bank account via NEFT/RTGS etc.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2097,\"title\":\"Can Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) invest in mutual funds?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Yes, non-resident Indians can invest in mutual funds in India. Please refer to the respective mutual fund’s offer documents for details.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2098,\"title\":\"Is it mandatory for the primary unit holder of the mutual fund folio be a joint holder in the bank account from which the payment for MF investments are made?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"The 1st holder in the mutual fund folio should be one of the joint holders in the registered bank account (though not necessarily be the primary holder in the bank account). In other words, the payment made from the bank account of the 2nd or 3rd holder in the MF folio is acceptable, only if the 1st

mutual funds in India. Please refer to the respective mutual fund’s offer documents for details.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2098,\"title\":\"Is it mandatory for the primary unit holder of the mutual fund folio be a joint holder in the bank account from which the payment for MF investments are made?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"The 1st holder in the mutual fund folio should be one of the joint holders in the registered bank account (though not necessarily be the primary holder in the bank account). In other words, the payment made from the bank account of the 2nd or 3rd holder in the MF folio is acceptable, only if the 1st holder in the MF folio is also one of the joint holders in the bank account; not otherwise.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"As per extant regulations, AMCs/RTAs are required to validate the bank accounts of the MF investors, which is typically done through “penny drop” mechanism via Immediate Payment Service (IMPS).\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"However, currently validation of bank account via IMPS is possible only in respect of the First Holder in the bank account. i.e., validation of the bank account through IMPS is not possible if the first holder in MF folio is the 2nd or 3rd holder in the bank account. Hence, in such cases, the MF investor will have to provide an alternate documentary proof such a cancelled cheque leaf /copy of bank passbook or bank statement etc., to enable the AMC to verify the correctness of the bank account.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2099,\"title\":\"How much time does it take to get my money back i.e., repurchase proceeds?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"As per recent SEBI circular SEBI/HO/IMD/IMD-I DOF2/P/CIR/2022/1 dated November 25, 2022, SEBI has revised Timelines for transfer of redemption proceeds to unitholders . Accordingly, the transfer of redemption or repurchase proceeds to the unitholders shall be made within three working days from the date of redemption or repurchase.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"For schemes investing atleast 80% of total assets in permissible overseas investments (Vide SEBI Circular SEBI/IMD/CIR No.7/104753/07 dated September 26, 2007, a list of permissible investments was prescribed for the purpose of overseas investments), the transfer of redemption or repurchase proceeds to the unitholders shall be made within five working days from the date of redemption or repurchase.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"In case of failure to dispatch the redemption/repurchase proceeds within 3 working days or 5 working days as the case may be, the AMC is liable to pay interest @15% for the period of delay beyond 3 or 5 working days.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"For list of exceptional situations and additional timelines for redemption payment - \",\"type\":\"text\"},{\"url\":\"https://www.amfiindia.com/Themes/Theme1/downloads/Listofexceptionalsituations.pdf\",\"type\":\"link\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Click here\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2100,\"title\":\"Where can I check if there is any unclaimed dividend or redemption amount against my name outstanding with a mutual fund?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"All Mutual Funds provide, the list of names and addresses of investors in whose folios there are unclaimed amounts (dividend/redemption) on their websites. The website of Mutual Funds is also provide information on the process of claiming the unclaimed amount and the necessary forms/documents required for the same.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"The information on unclaimed amount along with its prevailing value (based on income earned on deployment of such unclaimed amount) is separately disclosed to investors through the periodic statement of accounts/CAS sent to the investors.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2101,\"title\":\"Can an investor appoint a nominee for his investment in units of a mutual fund?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Yes. All Mutual Funds provide a nomination facility to individual investors.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"For more information regarding, please refer to the section titled “Nomination” .\",\"type\":\"text\",\"italic\":true}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.accordion\",\"id\":2102,\"title\":\"If mutual fund scheme is wound up, what happens to investors’ money?\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"In case of winding up of a scheme, the mutual funds refund to the unitholders whose names appear in the Unit Holders’ Register value of their outstanding Units at prevailing NAV, after adjustment of all expenses. Unitholders are entitled to receive a report on winding up from the mutual fund

accounts/CAS sent to the investors.","type":"text"}]}]},{"__component":"content.accordion","id":2101,"title":"Can an investor appoint a nominee for his investment in units of a mutual fund?","description":[{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"Yes. All Mutual Funds provide a nomination facility to individual investors.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"For more information regarding, please refer to the section titled “Nomination” .","type":"text","italic":true}]}]},{"__component":"content.accordion","id":2102,"title":"If mutual fund scheme is wound up, what happens to investors’ money?","description":[{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"In case of winding up of a scheme, the mutual funds refund to the unitholders whose names appear in the Unit Holders’ Register value of their outstanding Units at prevailing NAV, after adjustment of all expenses. Unitholders are entitled to receive a report on winding up from the mutual fund which gives all necessary details.","type":"text"}]}]},{"__component":"content.accordion","id":2103,"title":"How can investors redress their complaints?","description":[{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"The name of the Investor Relations Officer / contact person is mentioned in the Scheme Information Document (SID) of the mutual fund scheme, and also on the website of the concerned mutual fund, whom one may approach / write to in case of any query, complaints or grievance.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"","type":"text"}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"Investors should approach the concerned Mutual Fund / Investor Service Centre of the Mutual Fund in case of any complaint. If the complaint remains unresolved, the investor may approach SEBI by logging their complaint on SEBI’s complaints redressal system. Please log on to the section titled ‘SCORES” for more information.","type":"text"}]}]},{"__component":"content.accordion","id":2104,"title":"What is Total Return Index?","description":[{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"Total return index (TRI) is an index that measures the performance of a group of components (say, equities or debt instruments) by assuming that all cash distributions are reinvested, in addition to tracking the components’ price movements. In other words, it measures performance, reflecting the actual rate of return of an investment or a pool of investments over a given period. And it includes interest, capital gains, dividends and distributions realised over a given period of time from such pool of investments. Globally, TRI is viewed as a strong measure to reflect the actual out-performance over benchmark returns by a mutual fund scheme.","type":"text"}]}]},{"__component":"content.accordion","id":2105,"title":"How to protect yourself against unethical practices, frauds and scams?","description":[{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"Please click here ","type":"text"},{"url":"https://www.amfiindia.com/investor-corner/investor-center/Frauds_MFAwarenessBooklet.pdf","type":"link","children":[{"text":"MF Awareness Booklet","type":"text"}]},{"text":"","type":"text"}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"","type":"text"}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"","type":"text"}]}]}]},"zoneName":"InvestorService"}]

.css-6sg3a{margin:0;font-size:1.25rem;font-weight:700;color:#004739;line-height:1.2;color:#004739;text-transform:uppercase;}@media classification .css-wep8yw{margin:0;font-size:0.875rem!important;font-weight:600;color:#FFFFFF;line-height:1.2;font-family:'Montserrat','Montserrat Fallback';color:#555555;}@media (min-width:0px){.css-wep8yw{margin-bottom:8px;font-size:14px;}}@media (min-width:900px){.css-wep8yw{margin-bottom:16px;font-size:16px;}} Mutual funds come in many varieties, designed to meet different investor goals. Mutual funds can be broadly classified based on - Organisation Structure – Open ended, Close ended, Interval Management of Portfolio – Actively or Passively Investment Objective – Growth, Income, Liquidity Underlying Portfolio – Equity, Debt, Hybrid, Money market instruments, Multi Asset Thematic / solution oriented – Tax saving, Retirement benefit, Child welfare, Arbitrage Exchange Traded Funds Overseas funds Fund of funds Scheme Classification by Organization Structure • Open-ended schemes are perpetual, and open for subscription and repurchase on a continuous basis on all business days at the current NAV. • Close-ended schemes have a fixed maturity date. The units are issued at the time of the initial offer and redeemed only on maturity. The units of close-ended schemes are mandatorily listed to provide exit route before maturity and can be sold/traded on the stock exchanges. • Interval schemes allow purchase and redemption during specified transaction periods (intervals). The transaction period has to be for a minimum of 2 days and there should be at least a 15-day gap between two transaction periods. The units of interval schemes are also mandatorily listed on the stock exchanges. Scheme Classification by Portfolio Management .css-1ldo5ed{margin:0;font-size:0.875rem!important;font-weight:600;color:#FFFFFF;line-height:1.2;font-family:'Montserrat','Montserrat Fallback';color:rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.87);}@media (min-width:0px){.css-1ldo5ed{margin-bottom:8px;}}@media (min-width:900px){.css-1ldo5ed{margin-bottom:16px;}} Active Funds In an Active Fund, the Fund Manager is ‘Active’ in deciding whether to Buy, Hold, or Sell the underlying securities and in stock selection. Active funds adopt different strategies and styles to create and manage the portfolio. The investment strategy and style are described upfront in the Scheme Information document (offer document) Active funds expect to generate better returns (alpha) than the benchmark index. The risk and return in the fund will depend upon the strategy adopted. Active funds implement strategies to ‘select’ the stocks for the portfolio. Passive Funds Passive Funds hold a portfolio that replicates a stated Index or Benchmark e.g. – Index Funds Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) In a Passive Fund, the fund manager has a passive role, as the stock selection / Buy, Hold, Sell decision is driven by the Benchmark Index and the fund manager / dealer merely needs to replicate the same with minimal tracking error. Active v/s Passive Funds Active Fund – Rely on professional fund managers who manage investments. Aim to outperform Benchmark Index Suited for investors who wish to take advantage of fund managers' alpha generation potential. Passive Funds – Investment holdings

Funds hold a portfolio that replicates a stated Index or Benchmark e.g. – Index Funds Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) In a Passive Fund, the fund manager has a passive role, as the stock selection / Buy, Hold, Sell decision is driven by the Benchmark Index and the fund manager / dealer merely needs to replicate the same with minimal tracking error. Active v/s Passive Funds Active Fund – Rely on professional fund managers who manage investments. Aim to outperform Benchmark Index Suited for investors who wish to take advantage of fund managers' alpha generation potential. Passive Funds – Investment holdings mirror and closely track a benchmark index, e.g., Index Funds or Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) Suited for investors who want to allocate exactly as per market index. Lower Expense ratio hence lower costs to investors and better liquidity Classification by Investment Objectives Mutual funds offer products that cater to the different investment objectives of the investors such as – Capital Appreciation (Growth) Capital Preservation Regular Income Liquidity Tax-Saving Mutual funds also offer investment plans, such as Growth and Dividend options, to help tailor the investment to the investors’ needs. Growth funds Growth Funds are schemes that are designed to provide capital appreciation. Primarily invest in growth oriented assets, such as equity Investment in growth-oriented funds require a medium to long-term investment horizon. Historically, Equity as an asset class has outperformed most other kind of investments held over the long term. However, returns from Growth funds tend to be volatile over the short-term since the prices of the underlying equity shares may change. Hence investors must be able to take volatility in the returns in the short-term. Income funds The objective of Income Funds is to provide regular and steady income to investors. Income funds invest in fixed income securities such as Corporate Bonds, Debentures and Government securities. The fund’s return is from the interest income earned on these investments as well as capital gains from any change in the value of the securities. The fund will distribute the income provided the portfolio generates the required returns. There is no guarantee of income. The returns will depend upon the tenor and credit quality of the securities held. Liquid / Overnight /Money Market Mutual Funds Liquid Schemes, Overnight Funds and Money market mutual fund are investment options for investors seeking liquidity and principal protection, with commensurate returns. – The funds invest in money market instruments* with maturities not exceeding 91 days. – The return from the funds will depend upon the short-term interest rate prevalent in the market. These are ideal for investors who wish to park their surplus funds for short periods. – Investors who use these funds for longer holding periods may be sacrificing better returns possible from products suitable for a longer holding period. * Money Market Instruments includes commercial papers, commercial bills, treasury bills, Government securities having an unexpired maturity up to one year, call or notice money, certificate of deposit, usance bills, and any other like instruments as specified by the Reserve Bank of India from time to time. Classification by Investment Portfolio Mutual fund products can be classified based on their underlying portfolio composition – The first level of categorization will be on the basis of the asset class the fund invests in, such as equity / debt / money market instruments or gold. – The second level of categorization is on the basis of strategies and styles used to create the portfolio, such as, Income fund, Dynamic Bond Fund, Infrastructure fund, Large-cap/Mid-cap/Small-cap Equity fund, Value fund, etc. – The portfolio composition flows out of the

as specified by the Reserve Bank of India from time to time. Classification by Investment Portfolio Mutual fund products can be classified based on their underlying portfolio composition – The first level of categorization will be on the basis of the asset class the fund invests in, such as equity / debt / money market instruments or gold. – The second level of categorization is on the basis of strategies and styles used to create the portfolio, such as, Income fund, Dynamic Bond Fund, Infrastructure fund, Large-cap/Mid-cap/Small-cap Equity fund, Value fund, etc. – The portfolio composition flows out of the investment objectives of the scheme. The units are issued at the time of the initial offer and redeemed only on maturity. The units of close-ended schemes are mandatorily listed to provide exit route before maturity and can be sold/traded on the stock exchanges.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"•\",\"type\":\"text\"},{\"bold\":true,\"text\":\" Interval schemes\",\"type\":\"text\"},{\"text\":\" allow purchase and redemption during specified transaction periods (intervals). The transaction period has to be for a minimum of 2 days and there should be at least a 15-day gap between two transaction periods. The units of interval schemes are also mandatorily listed on the stock exchanges.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.related-topics\",\"id\":5659,\"title\":\"Categorization of MF Classification by Portfolio Management\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"heading\",\"level\":5,\"children\":[{\"bold\":true,\"text\":\"Active Funds\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"In an Active Fund, the Fund Manager is ‘Active’ in deciding whether to Buy, Hold, or Sell the underlying securities and in stock selection. Active funds adopt different strategies and styles to create and manage the portfolio.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list\",\"format\":\"unordered\",\"children\":[{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"The investment strategy and style are described upfront in the Scheme Information document (offer document)\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Active funds expect to generate better returns (alpha) than the benchmark index.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"The risk and return in the fund will depend upon the strategy adopted.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Active funds implement strategies to ‘select’ the stocks for the portfolio.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"type\":\"heading\",\"level\":5,\"children\":[{\"bold\":true,\"text\":\"Passive Funds\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Passive Funds hold a portfolio that replicates a stated Index or Benchmark e.g. –\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list\",\"format\":\"unordered\",\"children\":[{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Index Funds\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs)\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"In a Passive Fund, the fund manager has a passive role, as the stock selection / Buy, Hold, Sell decision is driven by the Benchmark Index and the fund manager / dealer merely needs to replicate the same with minimal tracking error.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.section\",\"id\":6223,\"title\":\"Active v/s Passive Funds\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Active Fund –\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list\",\"format\":\"unordered\",\"children\":[{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Rely on professional fund managers who manage investments.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Aim to outperform Benchmark Index\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Suited for investors who wish to take advantage of fund managers' alpha generation potential.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Passive Funds –\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list\",\"format\":\"unordered\",\"children\":[{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Investment holdings mirror and closely track a benchmark index, e.g., Index Funds or Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs)\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Suited for investors who want to allocate exactly as per market index.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Lower Expense ratio hence lower costs to investors and better liquidity\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.section\",\"id\":6224,\"title\":\"Classification by Investment Objectives\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Mutual funds offer products that cater to the different investment objectives of the investors such as –\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list\",\"format\":\"ordered\",\"children\":[{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Capital Appreciation (Growth)\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Capital Preservation\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Regular Income\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Liquidity\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Tax-Saving\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"type\":\"paragraph\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Mutual funds also offer investment plans, such as Growth and Dividend options, to help tailor the investment to the investors’ needs.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.section\",\"id\":6225,\"title\":\"Growth funds\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"list\",\"format\":\"unordered\",\"children\":[{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Growth Funds are schemes that are designed to provide capital appreciation.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Primarily invest in growth oriented assets, such as equity\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Investment in growth-oriented funds require a medium to long-term investment horizon.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Historically, Equity as an asset class has outperformed most other kind of investments held over the long term. However, returns from Growth funds tend to be volatile over the short-term since the prices of the underlying equity shares may change.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Hence investors must be able to take volatility in the returns in the short-term.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]}]}]},{\"__component\":\"content.section\",\"id\":6226,\"title\":\"Income funds\",\"description\":[{\"type\":\"list\",\"format\":\"unordered\",\"children\":[{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"The objective of Income Funds is to provide regular and steady income to investors.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"Income funds invest in fixed income securities such as Corporate Bonds, Debentures and Government securities.\",\"type\":\"text\"}]},{\"type\":\"list-item\",\"children\":[{\"text\":\"The fund’s return is from the interest income earned on these investments as well as capital gains from any change in

Equity as an asset class has outperformed most other kind of investments held over the long term. However, returns from Growth funds tend to be volatile over the short-term since the prices of the underlying equity shares may change.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"list-item","children":[{"text":"Hence investors must be able to take volatility in the returns in the short-term.","type":"text"}]}]}]},{"__component":"content.section","id":6226,"title":"Income funds","description":[{"type":"list","format":"unordered","children":[{"type":"list-item","children":[{"text":"The objective of Income Funds is to provide regular and steady income to investors.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"list-item","children":[{"text":"Income funds invest in fixed income securities such as Corporate Bonds, Debentures and Government securities.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"list-item","children":[{"text":"The fund’s return is from the interest income earned on these investments as well as capital gains from any change in the value of the securities.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"list-item","children":[{"text":"The fund will distribute the income provided the portfolio generates the required returns. There is no guarantee of income.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"list-item","children":[{"text":"The returns will depend upon the tenor and credit quality of the securities held.","type":"text"}]}]}]},{"__component":"content.section","id":6227,"title":"Liquid / Overnight /Money Market Mutual Funds","description":[{"type":"list","format":"unordered","children":[{"type":"list-item","children":[{"text":"Liquid Schemes, Overnight Funds and Money market mutual fund are investment options for investors seeking liquidity and principal protection, with commensurate returns.","type":"text"}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"– The funds invest in money market instruments* with maturities not exceeding 91 days.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"– The return from the funds will depend upon the short-term interest rate prevalent in the market.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"list","format":"unordered","children":[{"type":"list-item","children":[{"text":"These are ideal for investors who wish to park their surplus funds for short periods.","type":"text"}]}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"– Investors who use these funds for longer holding periods may be sacrificing better returns possible from products suitable for a longer holding period.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"","type":"text"}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"* Money Market Instruments includes commercial papers, commercial bills, treasury bills, Government securities having an unexpired maturity up to one year, call or notice money, certificate of deposit, usance bills, and any other like instruments as specified by the Reserve Bank of India from time to time.","type":"text"}]}]},{"__component":"content.section","id":6228,"title":"Classification by Investment Portfolio","description":[{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"Mutual fund products can be classified based on their underlying portfolio composition","type":"text"}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"– The first level of categorization will be on the basis of the asset class the fund invests in, such as equity / debt / money market instruments or gold.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"– The second level of categorization is on the basis of strategies and styles used to create the portfolio, such as, Income fund, Dynamic Bond Fund, Infrastructure fund, Large-cap/Mid-cap/Small-cap Equity fund, Value fund, etc.","type":"text"}]},{"type":"paragraph","children":[{"text":"– The portfolio composition flows out of the investment objectives of the scheme.","type":"text"}]}]}]}}]

","inLanguage":"en","url":"https://www.hdfcfund.com/explore/mutual-funds/hdfc-elss-tax-saver/direct","isPartOf":{"@type":"Website","@id":"https://www.hdfcfund.com/#website","name":"HDFC Mutual Fund","url":"https://www.hdfcfund.com/","inLanguage":"en","potentialAction":{"@type":"SearchAction","target":"https://www.hdfcfund.com/global-search?search={search_term}","query-input":"required name=search_term"}}} {"@context":"https://schema.org","@type":"InvestmentFund","name":"HDFC ELSS Tax Saver","description":"An Open-ended Equity Linked Savings Scheme with a statutory lock in of 3 years and tax benefit.

This is a simple and performing scheme which is eligible for distribution by new cadre of distributors.

The scheme offers the dual benefit of tax saving and wealth creation. It comes with a lock-in period of three years and provides individuals/HUFs a deduction from gross total income for investments in Equity-Linked Savings Scheme upto ₹1.5 lakh under section 80C of the Income Tax Act 1961.

The fund invests 80-100% of its portfolio in equity or equity related instruments.The equity strategy aims to provide long term capital appreciation from a reasonably diversified portfolio consisting of mid cap and large cap companies which:


	are likely to achive above average growth than the industry,
	enjoy distinct competetive advantages,
	have superior financial strenghts.

How to Invest in HDFC ELSS Tax Saver?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Investors can apply for their transactions requests either offline or electronically using the relevant application / transaction request forms available on our website or at any of our Officials Points of Acceptance.The application form/transaction slip for subscription/ redemption/ switches can be submitted at our Official Points of Acceptances whose addresses are available on the website of the AMC. These include:1. AMC / RTA’s branches i.e. Investor Services Centres*2. HDFC MF website and App/ RTA website for Investors to transact3. MFSS/BSE StAR MF/NMF II platforms of the Stock Exchanges(s)4. Authorized Points of Service of MF Utilities India Private Limited (MFUI)5. Channel partners/ Distributors/ RIAs/ Portfolio Managers/Execution Only Platforms (EOPs) who have tied up with the AMC6. MF Central * Note: Business Centres i.e. Sales offices of HDFC AMC are not Official Points of Acceptance of transactions.Please refer offer documents for more details. "}},{"@type":"Question","name":"2. Can I invest in SIP and Lump Sum of the HDFC ELSS Tax Saver?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Yes, SIP as well as Lump Sum investments are permitted with the minimum application amount being ₹500. text: Forms & Circulars MOA & AOA Distributor Corner Login Digital Onboarding Forms & Disclosures One Click Link FAQs I n v e s t / L o g i n Font Size -A A +A Mode Switcher Light Dark Text Magnifier On Off Reading Line On Off Home > Mutual Funds > ELSS Tax Saver HDFC ELSS Tax Saver Tax Saver DIRECT REGULAR An Open-ended Equity Linked Savings Scheme with a statutory lock in of 3 years and tax benefit. This is a simple and performing scheme which is eligible for distribution by new cadre of distributors. The scheme offers the dual benefit of tax saving and wealth creation. It comes with a lock-in period of three years and provides individuals/HUFs a deduction from gross total income for investments in Equity-Linked Savings Scheme upto ₹1.5 lakh under section 80C of the Income Tax Act 1961. The fund invests 80-100% of its portfolio in equity or equity related instruments.The equity strategy aims to provide long term capital appreciation from a reasonably diversified portfolio consisting of mid cap and large cap companies which: are likely to achive above average growth than the industry, enjoy distinct competetive advantages, have superior financial strenghts.   Returns since inception 13.75% Inception Date 01/01/2013 Riskometer Very High Min SIP ₹ 500 Ideal for Wealth Creation 3 Years and above Entry Load An entry load is a fee charged to investors when they purchase units of a Mutual Fund scheme. This fee was used to cover distribution and marketing expenses incurred by the Asset Management Company (AMC). Currently, Mutual Funds in India do not levy entry loads. NA TER TER The Total Expense Ration (TER) represents the costs for running and managing a Mutual Fund scheme. It is calculated as a percentage of the scheme’s average Net Asset Value (NAV). The daily NAV of a Mutual Fund is disclosed after deducting these expenses. Disclaimer Including Additional Expenses and Goods and Service Tax on Management Fees, if any. Click here to view the Total Expense Ratio 1.38 Lock in A lock-in period is the fixed duration during which investors cannot sell or redeem their investments/Mutual Fund units. For example, Equity Linked Savings Schemes (ELSS) Funds have a 3-year lock-in. 3 years NAV NA Net Asset Value (NAV) per unit is the total market value of securities of a scheme minus liabilities divided by the total number of outstanding units on a given date. NA AUM (31/03/2026) Assets Under Management (AUM) is the total market value of all assets that a Mutual Fund manages on behalf of its investors. ₹14,615.19 Cr. Benchmark A benchmark is a standard market index used to compare a Fund’s performance. It helps investors know whether the Fund is performing better, worse, or similar to the market. FAQs 1. How to Invest in HDFC ELSS Tax Saver? Investors can apply for their transactions requests either offline or electronically using the relevant application / transaction request forms available on our website or at any of our Officials Points of Acceptance. The application form/transaction slip for subscription/ redemption/ switches can be submitted at our Official Points of Acceptances whose addresses are available on the website of the AMC. These include: 1. AMC / RTA’s branches i.e. Investor Services Centres* 2. HDFC MF website and App/ RTA website for Investors to transact 3. MFSS/BSE StAR MF/NMF II platforms of the Stock Exchanges(s) 4. Authorized Points of Service of MF Utilities India Private Limited (MFUI) 5. Channel partners/ Distributors/ RIAs/ Portfolio Managers/Execution Only Platforms (EOPs) who have tied up with the AMC 6. MF Central  * Note: Business Centres i.e. Sales offices of HDFC AMC are not Official Points of Acceptance of transactions. Please refer offer documents for more details.   2. Can I invest in SIP and Lump Sum of the HDFC ELSS Tax Saver? Yes, SIP as well as Lump Sum investments are permitted with the minimum application amount being ₹500. Any additional purchase must have a minimum amount of ₹500. The SIP and Lumpsum are available in direct and regular plan with both Growth and IDCW option. 3. How to Redeem HDFC ELSS Tax Saver? Units of the scheme can be redeemed/switched out on any Business day at NAV based prices. For Redemption (including switch-out) applications • In respect of valid applications received upto 3 p.m. on a Business Day by the Fund, same day's closing NAV shall be applicable. • In respect of valid applications received after 3 p.m. on a Business Day by the Fund, the closing NAV of the next Business Day shall be applicable. Transactions through online facilities / electronic modes: The time of transaction done through various online facilities / electronic modes offered by the AMC, for the purpose of determining the applicability of NAV, would be the time when the request for purchase / sale / switch of units is received in the servers of AMC/RTA. The AMC has the right to amend cut off timings subject to SEBI (MF) Regulations for the smooth and efficient functioning of the Scheme.   OUR VISION To be the most respected asset manager in the world OUR MISSION To be the wealth creator for every Indian OUR VISION To be the most respected asset manager in the world OUR MISSION To be the wealth creator for every Indian OUR VISION To be the most respected asset manager in the world Address HDFC House, 2nd floor, 166, HT Parekh Marg, Churchgate, Mumbai, Maharashtra 400020, India Contact Us 180030106767

An Open-ended Equity Linked Savings Scheme with a statutory lock in of 3 years and tax benefit.

This is a simple and performing scheme which is eligible for distribution by new cadre of distributors.

The scheme offers the dual benefit of tax saving and wealth creation. It comes with a lock-in period of three years and provides individuals/HUFs a deduction from gross total income for investments in Equity-Linked Savings Scheme upto ₹1.5 lakh under section 80C of the Income Tax Act 1961.

The fund invests 80-100% of its portfolio in equity or equity related instruments.The equity strategy aims to provide long term capital appreciation from a reasonably diversified portfolio consisting of mid cap and large cap companies which:

  • are likely to achive above average growth than the industry,
  • enjoy distinct competetive advantages,
  • have superior financial strenghts.

Assets Under Management (AUM) is the total market value of all assets that a Mutual Fund manages on behalf of its investors.

investments in Equity-Linked Savings Scheme upto ₹1.5 lakh under section 80C of the Income Tax Act 1961.

The fund invests 80-100% of its portfolio in equity or equity related instruments.The equity strategy aims to provide long term capital appreciation from a reasonably diversified portfolio consisting of mid cap and large cap companies which:

  • are likely to achive above average growth than the industry,
  • enjoy distinct competetive advantages,
  • have superior financial strenghts.

Assets Under Management (AUM) is the total market value of all assets that a Mutual Fund manages on behalf of its investors.

","inceptionDate":"01/01/2013","entryLoad":"

An entry load is a fee charged to investors when they purchase units of a Mutual Fund scheme. This fee was used to cover distribution and marketing expenses incurred by the Asset Management Company (AMC). Currently, Mutual Funds in India do not levy entry loads.

","exitLoad":"

NIL

","terDirecct":"1.38","terRegular":"1.96","terinfo":"

TER

The Total Expense Ration (TER) represents the costs for running and managing a Mutual Fund scheme. It is calculated as a percentage of the scheme’s average Net Asset Value (NAV). The daily NAV of a Mutual Fund is disclosed after deducting these expenses.

Disclaimer

Including Additional Expenses and Goods and Service Tax on Management Fees, if any. Click here to view the Total Expense Ratio

","lockIn":"

A lock-in period is the fixed duration during which investors cannot sell or redeem their investments/Mutual Fund units. For example, Equity Linked Savings Schemes (ELSS) Funds have a 3-year lock-in.

","lockInValue":"3 years","entryLoadValue":null,"exitLoadValue":null,"navInfo":"

Net Asset Value (NAV) per unit is the total market value of securities of a scheme minus liabilities divided by the total number of outstanding units on a given date.

","benchmarkInfo":"

A benchmark is a standard market index used to compare a Fund’s performance. It helps investors know whether the Fund is performing better, worse, or similar to the market.

How to Invest in HDFC ELSS Tax Saver?","description":"

Investors can apply for their transactions requests either offline or electronically using the relevant application / transaction request forms available on our website or at any of our Officials Points of Acceptance.
The application form/transaction slip for subscription/ redemption/ switches can be submitted at our Official Points of Acceptances whose addresses are available on the website of the AMC. These include:
1. AMC / RTA’s branches i.e. Investor Services Centres*
2. HDFC MF website and App/ RTA website for Investors to transact
3. MFSS/BSE StAR MF/NMF II platforms of the Stock Exchanges(s)
4. Authorized Points of Service of MF Utilities India Private Limited (MFUI)
5. Channel partners/ Distributors/ RIAs/ Portfolio Managers/Execution Only Platforms (EOPs) who have tied up with the AMC
6. MF Central 

* Note: Business Centres i.e. Sales offices of HDFC AMC are not Official Points of Acceptance of transactions.
Please refer offer documents for more details.
 

"},{"title":"2. Can I invest in SIP and Lump Sum of the HDFC ELSS Tax Saver?","description":"

Yes, SIP as well as Lump Sum investments are permitted with the minimum application amount being ₹500. Any additional purchase must have a minimum amount of ₹500. The SIP and Lumpsum are available in direct and regular plan with both Growth and IDCW option.

"},{"title":"3. How to Redeem HDFC ELSS Tax Saver?","description":"

Units of the scheme can be redeemed/switched out on any Business day at NAV based prices.
For Redemption (including switch-out) applications
• In respect of valid applications received upto 3 p.m. on a Business Day by the Fund, same day's closing NAV shall be applicable.
• In respect of valid applications received after 3 p.m. on a Business Day by the Fund, the closing NAV of the next Business Day shall be applicable.


Transactions through online facilities / electronic modes:


The time of transaction done through various online facilities / electronic modes offered by the AMC, for the purpose of determining the applicability of NAV, would be the time when the request for purchase / sale / switch of units is received in the servers of AMC/RTA.
The AMC has the right to amend cut off timings subject to SEBI (MF) Regulations for the smooth and efficient functioning of the Scheme.

HDFC Flexi Cap Fund - Scheme Page
text: it is generally suited for investors with a long-term horizon (5+ years) who want diversified equity exposure."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Who should invest in HDFC Flexi Cap Fund?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Investors seeking long-term capital appreciation, diversification across market caps, and professional portfolio management may consider this fund."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Is this fund risky?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Since it invests in equities across market caps, the fund carries market risk. However, diversification across large, mid, and small caps aims to manage volatility."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"How is Flexi Cap different from Multi Cap funds?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Flexi Cap funds have complete flexibility in allocation across market caps, whereas Multi Cap funds follow SEBI-defined minimum allocation rules for each market segment."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"Can I invest through SIP in HDFC Flexi Cap Fund?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Yes, investors can start a SIP to invest regularly and benefit from rupee cost averaging over time."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What is the investment strategy of HDFC Flexi Cap Fund?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"The fund follows a diversified approach focusing on quality businesses, valuation discipline, and long-term growth opportunities across sectors and market caps."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What makes HDFC Flexi Cap Fund different from large-cap funds?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"Unlike large-cap funds that mainly invest in large cap companies, this fund can invest across the entire market spectrum, providing wider growth opportunities."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What is the minimum SIP amount in HDFC Flexi Cap Fund?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"The minimum SIP amount for HDFC Flexi Cap Fund is ₹100 (or as specified by the scheme). Investors can start a Systematic Investment Plan with small periodic investments and increase the amount over time based on their financial goals.You can refer website or the scheme information document for the latest investment requirements."}},{"@type":"Question","name":"What benchmark does HDFC Flexi Cap Fund use?","acceptedAnswer":{"@type":"Answer","text":"HDFC Flexi Cap Fund is benchmarked against NIFTY 500 Total Returns Index (TRI), which represents the performance of a broad range of companies across different market capitalisations."}}]} (function(w,d,s,l,i){ This adaptability makes HDFC Flexi Cap Fund attractive to investors seeking diversified exposure to the equity market. What is HDFC Flexi Cap Fund? Fund Description: HDFC Flexi Cap Fund is an open-ended equity mutual fund scheme that invests across large-cap, mid-cap, and small-cap companies. This allows the fund to combine: Stability from established market leaders Growth potential from mid-sized companies Emerging opportunities from smaller businesses helping create a balanced approach to long-term wealth creation. Who can consider HDFC Flexi Cap Fund? This fund may suit investors who: Have a long-term investment horizon (5+ years) Want diversified equity exposure across market caps Are comfortable with short-term market volatility for long-term growth How to start investing  Investors can start investing in HDFC Flexi Cap Fund through the following steps: Complete your KYC Keep PAN, Aadhaar and bank details ready. Visit HDFC Mutual Fund Website / App Go to the fund page and click Invest Now. Choose Investment Mode SIP – invest a fixed amount monthly Lump Sum – invest one time Select Plan & Option Direct or Regular Plan Growth or IDCW option Enter Amount & Payment Details Set SIP date or make payment online. Confirm & Start Investing Your investment will be processed and units will be allotted. Returns since inception 15.47% Inception Date 01/01/2013 Riskometer Very High Min SIP ₹ 100 Ideal for Wealth Creation 3 Years and above Entry Load An entry load is a fee charged to investors when they purchase units of a Mutual Fund scheme. This fee was used to cover distribution and marketing expenses incurred by the Asset Management Company (AMC). Currently, Mutual Funds in India do not levy entry loads. NA TER TER The Total Expense Ration (TER) represents the costs for running and managing a Mutual Fund scheme. It is calculated as a percentage of the scheme’s average Net Asset Value (NAV). The daily NAV of a Mutual Fund is disclosed after deducting these expenses. Disclaimer Including Additional Expenses and Goods and Service Tax on Management Fees, if any. FAQs What is HDFC Flexi Cap Fund? HDFC Flexi Cap Fund is an open-ended equity mutual fund that invests across large-cap, mid-cap, and small-cap stocks, giving the fund manager flexibility to capture opportunities across market segments. How does a Flexi Cap Fund work? A flexi cap fund dynamically allocates investments across different market capitalizations based on market conditions, valuations, and growth opportunities. What is the key advantage of a Flexi Cap Fund? The main advantage is flexibility — the fund manager can shift allocation between large, mid, and small caps to optimize risk-reward potential. Please refer Scheme Documents above for more details     Is HDFC Flexi Cap Fund suitable for long-term investment? Yes, it is generally suited for investors with a long-term horizon (5+ years) who want diversified equity exposure. Who should invest in HDFC Flexi Cap Fund? Investors seeking long-term capital appreciation, diversification across market caps, and professional portfolio management may consider this fund. Is this fund risky? Since it invests in equities across market caps, the fund carries market risk. However, diversification across large, mid,

source_name: HDFC Flexi Cap Fund - Scheme Page
text: the fund manager can shift allocation between large, mid, and small caps to optimize risk-reward potential. Please refer Scheme Documents above for more details     Is HDFC Flexi Cap Fund suitable for long-term investment? Yes, it is generally suited for investors with a long-term horizon (5+ years) who want diversified equity exposure. Who should invest in HDFC Flexi Cap Fund? Investors seeking long-term capital appreciation, diversification across market caps, and professional portfolio management may consider this fund. Is this fund risky? Since it invests in equities across market caps, the fund carries market risk. However, diversification across large, mid, and small caps aims to manage volatility. How is Flexi Cap different from Multi Cap funds? Flexi Cap funds have complete flexibility in allocation across market caps, whereas Multi Cap funds follow SEBI-defined minimum allocation rules for each market segment. Can I invest through SIP in HDFC Flexi Cap Fund? Yes, investors can start a SIP to invest regularly and benefit from rupee cost averaging over time. What is the investment strategy of HDFC Flexi Cap Fund? The fund follows a diversified approach focusing on quality businesses, valuation discipline, and long-term growth opportunities across sectors and market caps. What makes HDFC Flexi Cap Fund different from large-cap funds? Unlike large-cap funds that mainly invest in large cap companies, this fund can invest across the entire market spectrum, providing wider growth opportunities. What is the minimum SIP amount in HDFC Flexi Cap Fund? The minimum SIP amount for HDFC Flexi Cap Fund is ₹100 (or as specified by the scheme). Investors can start a Systematic Investment Plan with small periodic investments and increase the amount over time based on their financial goals. You can refer website or the scheme information document for the latest investment requirements. What benchmark does HDFC Flexi Cap Fund use? HDFC Flexi Cap Fund is benchmarked against NIFTY 500 Total Returns Index (TRI), which represents the performance of a broad range of companies across different market capitalisations. ","inceptionDate":"01/01/2013","entryLoad":"

An entry load is a fee charged to investors when they purchase units of a Mutual Fund scheme. This fee was used to cover distribution and marketing expenses incurred by the Asset Management Company (AMC). Currently, Mutual Funds in India do not levy entry loads.

","exitLoad":"
  • In respect of each purchase / switch-in of Units, an Exit Load of 1.00% is payable if Units are redeemed / switched-out within 1 year from the date of allotment.
  • No Exit Load is payable if Units are redeemed / switched-out after 1 year from the date of allotment.
","terDirecct":"0.97","terRegular":"1.57","terinfo":"

TER

The Total Expense Ration (TER) represents the costs for running and managing a Mutual Fund scheme. It is calculated as a percentage of the scheme’s average Net Asset Value (NAV). The daily NAV of a Mutual Fund is disclosed after deducting these expenses.

Disclaimer

Including Additional Expenses and Goods and Service Tax on Management Fees, if any. Click here to view the Total Expense Ratio

","lockIn":"

A lock-in period is the fixed duration during which investors cannot sell or redeem their investments/Mutual Fund units. For example, Equity Linked Savings Schemes (ELSS) Funds have a 3-year lock-in.

","lockInValue":null,"entryLoadValue":null,"exitLoadValue":null,"navInfo":"

Net Asset Value (NAV) per unit is the total market value of securities of a scheme minus liabilities divided by the total number of outstanding units on a given date.

","benchmarkInfo":"

A benchmark is a standard market index used to compare a Fund’s performance. It helps investors know whether the Fund is performing better, worse, or similar to the market.
