Please refer Scheme Documents above for more details\u003cbr\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u0026nbsp;\u003c/p\u003e"},{"title":"Is HDFC Flexi Cap Fund suitable for long-term investment?","description":"\u003cp\u003eYes, it is generally suited for investors with a long-term horizon (5+ years) who want diversified equity exposure.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"title":"Who should invest in HDFC Flexi Cap Fund?","description":"\u003cp\u003eInvestors seeking long-term capital appreciation, diversification across market caps, and professional portfolio management may consider this fund.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"title":"Is this fund risky?","description":"\u003cp\u003eSince it invests in equities across market caps, the fund carries market risk. However, diversification across large, mid, and small caps aims to manage volatility.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"title":"How is Flexi Cap different from Multi Cap funds?","description":"\u003cp\u003eFlexi Cap funds have complete flexibility in allocation across market caps, whereas Multi Cap funds follow SEBI-defined minimum allocation rules for each market segment.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"title":"Can I invest through SIP in HDFC Flexi Cap Fund?","description":"\u003cp\u003eYes, investors can start a SIP to invest regularly and benefit from rupee cost averaging over time.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"title":"What is the investment strategy of HDFC Flexi Cap Fund?","description":"\u003cp\u003eThe fund follows a diversified approach focusing on quality businesses, valuation discipline, and long-term growth opportunities across sectors and market caps.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"title":"What makes HDFC Flexi Cap Fund different from large-cap funds?","description":"\u003cp\u003eUnlike large-cap funds that mainly invest in large cap companies, this fund can invest across the entire market spectrum, providing wider growth opportunities.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"title":"What is the minimum SIP amount in HDFC Flexi Cap Fund?","description":"\u003cp\u003eThe minimum SIP amount for HDFC Flexi Cap Fund is ₹100 (or as specified by the scheme). Investors can start a Systematic Investment Plan with small periodic investments and increase the amount over time based on their financial goals.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003eYou can refer website or the scheme information document for the latest investment requirements.\u003c/p\u003e"},{"title":"What benchmark does HDFC Flexi Cap Fund use?","description":"\u003cp\u003eHDFC Flexi Cap Fund is benchmarked against NIFTY 500 Total Returns Index (TRI), which represents the performance of a broad range of companies across different market capitalisations.\u003c/p\u003e"}]},{"schemeDetail":null},{"productLabelling":{"title":"Product Labelling","values":"\u003cdiv class=\"drupal\"\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cspan\u003eThis product is suitable for investors who are seeking~\u003c/span\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cspan\u003e• To generate long-term capital appreciation / income\u003c/span\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cspan\u003e• Investment predominantly in equity \u0026amp; equity related instruments\u003c/span\u003e\u003cbr\u003e\u003cspan\u003e~Investors should consult their financial advisers if in doubt about whether the product is suitable for them.\u003c/span\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003c/div\u003e"},"productLabellingImage":null,"benchmarkRiskometer":{"title":"Benchmark Riskometer","values":"\u003cdiv class=\"drupal\"\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://files.hdfcfund.com/s3fs-public/inline-images/Riskometer_Benchmark_Very%20High_53.jpg\" data-entity-uuid=\"527c1684-e593-416d-8fd9-cee15de58b1f\" data-entity-type=\"file\" alt=\"HDFC Flexi Cap Fund \" width=\"250\" height=\"180\"\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003c/div\u003e"},"potentialRiskClass":{"title":"Potential Risk Class Matrix (i.e. Maximum interest rate risk / credit risk a debt scheme can take)","values":null}}],"meta":{"title":"HDFC Flexi Cap Fund to Invest - Direct Plan","description":"Explore the HDFC Flexi Cap Fund Direct plan, a diversified equity fund that

The aim of equity strategy will be to predominantly build a portfolio of mid-cap companies which have:

b) sound financial strength

-A A +A Mode Switcher Light Dark Text Magnifier On Off Reading Line On Off Home > Mutual Funds > Mid Cap Fund HDFC Mid Cap Fund Equity DIRECT REGULAR An open ended equity scheme predominantly investing in mid cap stocks. The Scheme shall follow a predominantly Mid cap strategy with a minimum exposure of 65% to Mid-Cap stocks. The Scheme may also seek participation in other equity and equity related securities to achieve optimal portfolio construction. The aim of equity strategy will be to predominantly build a portfolio of mid-cap companies which have: a) reasonable growth prospects b) sound financial strength c) sustainable business models d) acceptable valuation that offers potential for capital appreciation.   Returns since inception 19.51% Inception Date 01/01/2013 Riskometer Very High Min SIP ₹ 100 Ideal for Wealth Creation 3 Years and above Entry Load An entry load is a fee charged to investors when they purchase units of a Mutual Fund scheme. This fee was used to cover distribution and marketing expenses incurred by the Asset Management Company (AMC). Currently, Mutual Funds in India do not levy entry loads. NA TER TER The Total Expense Ration (TER) represents the costs for running and managing a Mutual Fund scheme. It is calculated as a percentage of the scheme’s average Net Asset Value (NAV). The daily NAV of a Mutual Fund is disclosed after deducting these expenses. Disclaimer Including Additional Expenses and Goods and Service Tax on Management Fees, if any. Click here to view the Total Expense Ratio 0.79 Lock in A lock-in period is the fixed duration during which investors cannot sell or redeem their investments/Mutual Fund units. For example, Equity Linked Savings Schemes (ELSS) Funds have a 3-year lock-in. NA NAV NA Net Asset Value (NAV) per unit is the

For example, Equity Linked Savings Schemes (ELSS) Funds have a 3-year lock-in. NA NAV NA Net Asset Value (NAV) per unit is the total market value of securities of a scheme minus liabilities divided by the total number of outstanding units on a given date. NA AUM (31/03/2026) Assets Under Management (AUM) is the total market value of all assets that a Mutual Fund manages on behalf of its investors. ₹85,357.92 Cr. Benchmark A benchmark is a standard market index used to compare a Fund’s performance. It helps investors know whether the Fund is performing better, worse, or similar to the market. No Entry/Exit Load shall be levied on bonus units and units allotted on dividend reinvestment Product Labelling Benchmark Riskometer This product is suitable for investors who are seeking~ • to generate long-term capital appreciation/income • investments predominantly in Mid-Cap companies ~Investors should consult their financial advisers if in doubt about whether the product is suitable for them. FAQs Can I invest in SIP and Lump Sum of HDFC Mid Cap Fund ? Yes, SIP as well as Lump Sum investments are permitted with the minimum application amount being ₹100. Any additional purchase must have a minimum amount of ₹100. The SIP and Lumpsum are available in direct and regular plan with both Growth and IDCW option.   How to Invest in HDFC Mid Cap Fund ? Investors can apply for their transactions requests either offline or electronically using the relevant application / transaction request forms available on our website or at any of our Officials Points of Acceptance. The application form/transaction slip for subscription/ redemption/ switches can be submitted at our Official Points of Acceptances whose addresses are available on the website of the AMC. These include: AMC / RTA’s branches i.e. Investor Services Centres* HDFC MF website and App/ RTA website for Investors to transact MFSS/BSE StAR MF/NMF II platforms of the Stock Exchanges(s) Authorized Points of Service of MF Utilities India Private Limited (MFUI) Channel partners/ Distributors/ RIAs/ Portfolio Managers/Execution Only

An open ended equity scheme predominantly investing in mid cap stocks.

The Scheme shall follow a predominantly Mid cap strategy with a minimum exposure of 65% to Mid-Cap stocks. The Scheme may also seek participation in other equity and equity related securities to achieve optimal portfolio construction.

The aim of equity strategy will be to predominantly build a portfolio of mid-cap companies which have:
a) reasonable growth prospects
b) sound financial strength
c) sustainable business models
d) acceptable valuation that offers potential for capital appreciation.

Assets Under Management (AUM) is the total market value of all assets that a Mutual Fund manages on behalf of its investors.

","inceptionDate":"01/01/2013","entryLoad":"

An entry load is a fee charged to investors when they purchase units of a Mutual Fund scheme. This fee was used to cover distribution and marketing expenses incurred by the Asset Management Company (AMC). Currently, Mutual Funds in India do not levy entry loads.

","exitLoad":"

● In respect of each purchase/switch-in of Units, an Exit Load of 1.00% is payable if Units are redeemed/switched-out within 1 year from the date of allotment.
● No Exit Load is payable if Units are redeemed/switched-out after 1 year from the date of allotment.
No Entry/Exit Load shall be levied on bonus units and units allotted on dividend reinvestment

","terDirecct":"0.79","terRegular":"1.36","terinfo":"

TER

The Total Expense Ration (TER) represents the costs for running and managing a Mutual Fund scheme. It is calculated as a percentage of the scheme’s average Net Asset Value (NAV). The daily NAV of a Mutual Fund is disclosed after deducting these expenses.

Disclaimer

Including Additional Expenses and Goods and Service Tax on Management Fees, if any. Click here to view the Total Expense Ratio

","lockIn":"

A lock-in period is the fixed duration during which investors cannot sell or redeem their investments/Mutual Fund units. For example, Equity Linked Savings Schemes (ELSS) Funds have a 3-year lock-in.

","lockInValue":null,"entryLoadValue":null,"exitLoadValue":null,"navInfo":"

Net Asset Value (NAV) per unit is the total market value of securities of a scheme minus liabilities divided by the total number of outstanding units on a given date.

","benchmarkInfo":"

A benchmark is a standard market index used to compare a Fund’s performance. It helps investors know whether the Fund is performing better, worse, or similar to the market.

Yes, SIP as well as Lump Sum investments are permitted with the minimum application amount being ₹100. Any additional purchase must have a minimum amount of ₹100. The SIP and Lumpsum are available in direct and regular plan with both Growth and IDCW option.
 

"},{"title":"How to Invest in HDFC Mid Cap Fund ?","description":"

Investors can apply for their transactions requests either offline or electronically using the relevant application / transaction request forms available on our website or at any of our Officials Points of Acceptance.

The application form/transaction slip for subscription/ redemption/ switches can be submitted at our Official Points of Acceptances whose addresses are available on the website of the AMC. These include:

  1. AMC / RTA’s branches i.e. Investor Services Centres*
  2. HDFC MF website and App/ RTA website for Investors to transact
  3. MFSS/BSE StAR MF/NMF II platforms of the Stock Exchanges(s)
  4. Authorized Points of Service of MF Utilities India Private Limited (MFUI)
  5. Channel partners/ Distributors/ RIAs/ Portfolio Managers/Execution Only Platforms (EOPs) who have tied up with the AMC
  6. MF Central
     

*Note: Business Centres i.e. Sales offices of HDFC AMC are not Official Points of Acceptance of transactions.
The above list is indicative. For further details, including cut-off timing and applicability of NAV, refer Section II.
Please refer Scheme Documents above for more details

text: Downloads Investor Awareness Programmes KYC & Redressal of Complaints About Us Corporate Profile HDFC MF at a Glance What Sets Us Apart Corporate Social Responsibility Environmental, Social and Governance Our Board and Trustees Press Releases AMC Shareholder Financial Information Corporate Governance Forms & Circulars MOA & AOA Distributor Corner Login Digital Onboarding Forms & Disclosures One Click Link FAQs I n v e s t / L o g i n Font Size -A A +A Mode Switcher Light Dark Text Magnifier On Off Reading Line On Off Home > Mutual Funds > Small Cap Fund HDFC Small Cap Fund Equity DIRECT REGULAR An open ended equity scheme predominantly investing in small cap stocks Today’s Beginners Could Be Tomorrow’s Leaders What is HDFC Small Cap Fund? HDFC Small Cap Fund is an open-ended equity mutual fund scheme that predominantly invests in small-cap companies. Small-cap companies typically represent emerging businesses with strong growth potential. By investing in a diversified portfolio of small-cap stocks, the fund aims to identify companies with scalable business models and participate in their long-term growth journey. Investment Objective is to provide long-term capital appreciation/ income by investing predominantly in small-cap companies. There is no assurance that the investment objective of the scheme will be achieved. Investment Strategy of HDFC Small Cap Fund HDFC Small Cap Fund focuses on identifying high-quality small-cap companies with long-term growth potential trading at reasonable valuations. The portfolio construction emphasizes: Bottom-up stock selection based on company fundamentals Focus on quality companies, with sound financial strength and reasonable return on equity Diversification across sectors and industries Invest at sensible valuations in companies trading at reasonable multiples Why invest in HDFC Small Cap Fund? Untapped Universe: With more than 1000 companies beyond the top 250 by market cap, the small-cap space offers a wider scope for alpha generation. Under-Researched = Opportunity: Many small-cap stocks are less tracked by analysts, allowing fund managers to identify mispriced winners early. Potential Re-rating: As small companies grow, they attract institutional interest and better coverage, often leading to higher valuations. Proven Strategy: The fund has a 17+ year track record of spotting scalable, quality small-cap businesses with sound management. Smart Diversification: Adds potential for high-growth to your portfolio, complementing large and mid-cap exposure of investors Who can consider HDFC Small Cap Fund? This fund may suit investors who: Seek long-term capital appreciation (5+ years) Are comfortable with short-term volatility Want exposure to emerging growth businesses Aim to diversify across market-cap segments How to start investing in HDFC Small Cap Fund? Investors can start investing in HDFC Small Cap Fund through the following steps: Complete your KYC Keep PAN, Aadhaar and bank details ready. Visit HDFC Mutual Fund Website / App Go to the fund page and click Invest Now. Choose Investment Mode SIP – invest a fixed amount monthly Lump Sum – invest one time Select Plan & Option Direct or Regular Plan Growth or IDCW option Enter Amount & Payment Details Set SIP date or make payment online. Confirm & Start Investing Your investment will be processed and units will be allotted. This fee was used to cover distribution and marketing expenses incurred by the Asset Management Company (AMC). Currently, Mutual Funds in India do not levy entry loads. NA TER TER The Total Expense Ration (TER) represents the costs for running and managing a Mutual Fund scheme. It is calculated as a percentage of the scheme’s average Net Asset Value (NAV). The daily NAV of a Mutual Fund is disclosed after deducting these expenses. Disclaimer Including Additional Expenses and Goods and Service Tax on Management Fees, if any. For example, Equity Linked Savings Schemes (ELSS) Funds have a 3-year lock-in. NA NAV NA Net Asset Value (NAV) per unit is the total market value of securities of a scheme minus liabilities divided by the total number of outstanding units on a given date. NA AUM (31/03/2026) Assets Under Management (AUM) is the total market value of all assets that a Mutual Fund manages on behalf of its investors. ₹33,724.28 Cr. Benchmark A benchmark is a standard market index used to compare a Fund’s performance. It helps investors know whether the Fund is performing better, worse, or similar to the market. A small-cap fund invests mainly in companies ranked beyond the top 250 by market capitalization. Is this fund risky? Small-cap funds tend to be more volatile but may offer higher growth potential over the long term. What is the ideal investment horizon? Typically 5 years or more. Can beginners invest? Yes, if they understand equity market volatility and invest for long-term goals. SIP or lump sum: which is better? SIP helps average

source_name: HDFC Small Cap Fund - Scheme Page
text: in Small-Cap companies   ~Investors should consult their financial advisers if in doubt about whether the product is suitable for them.   FAQs What is a small-cap fund? A small-cap fund invests mainly in companies ranked beyond the top 250 by market capitalization. Is this fund risky? Small-cap funds tend to be more volatile but may offer higher growth potential over the long term. What is the ideal investment horizon? Typically 5 years or more. Can beginners invest? Yes, if they understand equity market volatility and invest for long-term goals. SIP or lump sum: which is better? SIP helps average costs and reduce timing risk. Does the fund only invest in small caps? Primarily yes, but it can allocate a portion to large and mid caps for flexibility. Is this suitable for retirement planning? It can form part of a diversified long-term retirement portfolio but is typically not a standalone option. What role does this fund play in a portfolio? It aims to enhance long-term growth potential within a diversified equity portfolio. What benchmark does HDFC Small Cap Fund track? HDFC Small Cap Fund is benchmarked against BSE 250 SmallCap Index (TRI) What is the minimum investment in HDFC Small Cap Fund? Rs.100/- and any amount thereafter How is HDFC Small Cap Fund different from a large-cap fund? A small-cap fund invests primarily in smaller companies, whereas large-cap funds invest mainly in well-established companies with larger market capitalisation. Small-cap funds may offer higher growth potential but can also experience higher volatility. Small-cap companies typically represent emerging businesses with strong growth potential.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003eBy investing in a diversified portfolio of small-cap stocks, the fund aims to identify companies with scalable business models and participate in their long-term growth journey.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eInvestment Objective is to\u003c/strong\u003e provide long-term capital appreciation/ income by investing predominantly in small-cap companies. There is no assurance that the investment objective of the scheme will be achieved.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eInvestment Strategy of HDFC Small Cap Fund\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003eHDFC Small Cap Fund focuses on identifying high-quality small-cap companies with long-term growth potential trading at reasonable valuations.\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003eThe portfolio construction emphasizes:\u003c/p\u003e\u003cul\u003e\u003cli\u003eBottom-up stock selection based on company fundamentals\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003eFocus on quality companies, with sound financial strength and reasonable return on equity\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003eDiversification across sectors and industries\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003eInvest at sensible valuations in companies trading at reasonable multiples\u003c/li\u003e\u003c/ul\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWhy invest in HDFC Small Cap Fund?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cul\u003e\u003cli\u003eUntapped Universe: With more than 1000 companies beyond the top 250 by market cap, the small-cap space offers a wider scope for alpha generation.\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003eUnder-Researched = Opportunity: Many small-cap stocks are less tracked by analysts, allowing fund managers to identify mispriced winners early.\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003ePotential Re-rating: As small companies grow, they attract institutional interest and better coverage, often leading to higher valuations.\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003eProven Strategy: The fund has a 17+ year track record of spotting scalable, quality small-cap businesses with sound management.\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003eSmart Diversification: Adds potential for high-growth to your portfolio, complementing large and mid-cap exposure of investors\u003c/li\u003e\u003c/ul\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eWho can consider HDFC Small Cap Fund?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003eThis fund may suit investors who:\u003c/p\u003e\u003cul\u003e\u003cli\u003eSeek long-term capital appreciation (5+ years)\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003eAre comfortable with short-term volatility\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003eWant exposure to emerging growth businesses\u003c/li\u003e\u003cli\u003eAim to diversify across market-cap segments\u003c/li\u003e\u003c/ul\u003e\u003cp\u003e\u003cstrong\u003eHow to start investing in HDFC Small Cap Fund?\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/p\u003e\u003cp\u003eInvestors can start investing in HDFC Small Cap Fund through the following steps:\u003c/p\u003e\u003col\u003e\u003cli\u003eComplete     An entry load is a fee charged to investors when they purchase units of a Mutual Fund scheme. This fee was used to cover distribution and marketing expenses incurred by the Asset Management Company (AMC). Currently, Mutual Funds in India do not levy entry loads.

    ","exitLoad":"
    • In respect of each purchase / switch-in of Units, an Exit Load of 1.00% is payable if Units are redeemed / switched out within 1 year from the date of allotment.
    • No Exit Load is payable if Units are redeemed / switched out after 1 year from the date of allotment.

    TER

    The Total Expense Ration (TER) represents the costs for running and managing a Mutual Fund scheme. It is calculated as a percentage of the scheme’s average Net Asset Value (NAV). The daily NAV of a Mutual Fund is disclosed after deducting these expenses.

    Including Additional Expenses and Goods and Service Tax on Management Fees, if any. Click here to view the Total Expense Ratio

text: growth, diversified equity fund, hdfc small cap mutual fund, small cap funds, mutual fund\r\n","og_title":null,"og_description":null,"og_image":null,"og_url":null,"category":"Equity","fundDescription":"An open ended equity scheme predominantly investing in small cap stocksToday’s Beginners Could Be Tomorrow’s LeadersWhat is HDFC Small Cap Fund?HDFC Small Cap Fund is an open-ended equity mutual fund scheme that predominantly invests in small-cap companies. Small-cap companies typically represent emerging businesses with strong growth potential.By investing in a diversified portfolio of small-cap stocks, the fund aims to identify companies with scalable business models and participate in their long-term growth journey.Investment Objective is to provide long-term capital appreciation/ income by investing predominantly in small-cap companies. There is no assurance that the investment objective of the scheme will be achieved.Investment Strategy of HDFC Small Cap FundHDFC Small Cap Fund focuses on identifying high-quality small-cap companies with long-term growth potential trading at reasonable valuations.The portfolio construction emphasizes:Bottom-up stock selection based on company fundamentalsFocus on quality companies, with sound financial strength and reasonable return on equityDiversification across sectors and industriesInvest at sensible valuations in companies trading at reasonable multiplesWhy invest in HDFC Small Cap Fund?Untapped Universe: With more than 1000 companies beyond the top 250 by market cap, the small-cap space offers a wider scope for alpha generation.Under-Researched = Opportunity: Many small-cap stocks are less tracked by analysts, allowing fund managers to identify mispriced winners early.Potential Re-rating: As small companies grow, they attract institutional interest and better coverage, often leading to higher valuations.Proven Strategy: The fund has a 17+ year track record of spotting scalable, quality small-cap businesses with sound management.Smart Diversification: Adds potential for high-growth to your portfolio, complementing large and mid-cap exposure of investorsWho can consider HDFC Small Cap Fund?This fund may suit investors who:Seek long-term capital appreciation (5+ years)Are comfortable with short-term volatilityWant exposure to emerging growth businessesAim to diversify across market-cap segmentsHow to start investing in HDFC Small Cap Fund?Investors can start investing in HDFC Small Cap Fund through the following steps:Complete your KYCKeep PAN, Aadhaar and bank details ready.Visit HDFC Mutual Fund Website / AppGo to the fund page and click Invest Now.Choose Investment ModeSIP – invest a fixed amount monthlyLump Sum – invest one timeSelect Plan \u0026amp; OptionDirect or Regular PlanGrowth or IDCW optionEnter Amount \u0026amp; Payment DetailsSet SIP date or make payment online.Confirm \u0026amp; Start InvestingYour investment will be processed and units will be allotted.","fundTitle":"HDFC Small Cap Fund"}},"code":200,"message":"success"}}},"page":"/explore/mutual-funds/[overview]/[type]","query":{"overview":"hdfc-small-cap-fund","type":"direct"},"buildId":"qyalHwxMJuhxhGiqtZbNG","isFallback":false,"isExperimentalCompile":false,"dynamicIds":[4111],"gssp":true,"appGip":true,"scriptLoader":[]}

:: Personal Finance & Investment : Securities Market Investments : Let's Learn | SEBI Investor Home Personal Finance and Investment Investments Securities Market Investments : Let's Learn What you will Learn This section provides you an essential guide on another avenue for investment i.e. securities market that will help you grow your money and secure a better future for yourself and your family. This will cover a range of topics, including the basics of the securities market, investment products, how to review investments, and the do's and don'ts of investing. Additionally, we will provide guidance on grievance redressal. Who it is for It is suitable for everyone, regardless of their financial knowledge. However, it is particularly beneficial for those who are looking to invest in the securities market. Core Concepts Covered The basics of the securities market and the requirements for investing in the securities market. Features and benefits of various investment products, such as shares, mutual funds, ETFs, bonds, derivatives, REITs, and INVITs. The fundamental do's and don'ts of investing in the securities market. How to review your investments and ensure they align with your goals and risk tolerance. How to resolve disputes with your financial service provider.

:: Securities Market Investment: Understanding Mutual Funds | SEBI Investor :: Home Personal Finance and Investment Securities Market Investment Content Understanding Mutual Funds Understanding Mutual Funds In the article “Why one should invest”, we saw the need for investing one’s surplus money. The investments can be managed by oneself, or through professional help. Mutual fund is one such investment vehicle where any investor can avail the expert services of professional money managers. What is Mutual Fund? A mutual fund is a trust who collects/pools money from the various investors and invest the money in different types of securities. The investment portfolio managed by a professional organisation known as an asset management company. This company launches schemes (also known as products) by stating certain objectives clearly. An investor can invest into these schemes if the objective of the scheme is in line with the investor’s needs. By investing in a mutual fund scheme, the investor has outsourced the job of managing his money with the asset management company. This company would then: - Invest the investor’s money in line with the stated objective, Handle all the research required to select the underlying investments Keep a track of those investments Make changes to the portfolio, if required and Also handle the entire administrative job related to the above. It will summarise the performance of the portfolio into a statement and give it to the investor, who gets the complete picture on that one page – known as the account statement Advantages of investing in mutual funds: Professional management of funds: The mutual fund schemes are managed by professional fund managers. They are registered and regulated professionals. Low cost: The asset management company charges expenses towards management of the funds as well as paying other professionals that provide other services like fund accounting, custody of securities, auditing, distribution of the schemes, among others. These expenses are capped through regulations by SEBI. Diversified portfolio: The schemes must hold a diversified portfolio as mandated through SEBI regulations, except in certain cases. Transparency: Mutual funds must publish the scheme investment objective, upfront. The portfolio where money is invested is disclosed at regular intervals, as prescribed by the regulations. The accounting value of each unit, known as the NAV (Net Asset Value), must be published daily. All material changes in the scheme are to be communicated to the unit holders in a timely manner. Such transparency allows the unit holders (both existing and prospective) to take informed decisions. Convenience: A mutual fund investment is as easy to operate as a bank account, as on any working day, an investor can make additional investment in the same folio as well as redeem money as may be required. There are facilities to invest or redeem in a systematic manner – Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) and Systematic Withdrawal Plan (SWP). Strong regulatory framework: Mutual funds are regulated by SEBI through a strong regulatory framework. // Get the element with id="defaultOpen" and click on it document.getElementById("defaultOpen").click();
